thinking.thinking: Let me start by loading the context for the ponder 'human-run-uat' to understand what this idea is about.

input.command: sdlc ponder artifacts human-run-uat 2>&1 && sdlc ponder session list human-run-uat 2>&1
input.description: List artifacts and sessions for human-run-uat
Find all files in the human-run-uat ponder directory

input.command: sdlc milestone list 2>&1 && sdlc feature list 2>&1
input.description: List existing milestones and features to avoid duplication
Filter milestone list for relevant entries

thinking.thinking:
The idea is clear and well-scoped. It's a medium-sized feature that adds a human path for UAT submission at two surfaces:
1. Milestone UAT (`MilestonePreparePanel.tsx`)
2. Feature-level QA (`FeatureDetail.tsx`)

Looking at the existing milestones, the next version would be somewhere after v27. The existing milestones go up to v27-agentic-tool-suite. I should create a new milestone like `v28-human-run-uat` or similar.

The scope is:
- 2 new REST endpoints


- 1 new field on UatRun
- 1 shared UI modal
- UI changes in 2 components

I could structure this as either two features (backend and frontend) or three features (data model, endpoints, and UI). The two-feature approach seems cleaner: one for the backend changes including the new field and endpoints, and one for the frontend modal and button additions across both surfaces. I'm settling on two features instead—the backend work handling the data model and both endpoints, and the frontend covering the modal plus its integration into both surfaces.

text: The ponder is well-shaped: a medium-sized, single-concern feature. I'll create one milestone (`v28-human-run-uat`) with two features: backend changes and frontend UI.

input.command: sdlc milestone create v28-human-run-uat --title "Human-Run UAT — manual result submission" --vision "Give humans a peer path to AI for submitting UAT results from both milestone and feature QA surfaces" 2>&1
sdlc milestone create v28-human-run-uat --title "Human-Run UAT — manual result submission" 2>&1

sdlc feature create human-uat-backend --title "Human UAT backend: UatRun mode field and submission endpoints" 2>&1 && sdlc feature create human-uat-frontend --title "Human UAT frontend: submission modal and secondary buttons" 2>&1
input.description: Create both features for the milestone

input.command: sdlc milestone add-feature v28-human-run-uat human-uat-backend 2>&1 && sdlc milestone add-feature v28-human-run-uat human-uat-frontend 2>&1
input.description: Add both features to the milestone
Link ponder to the created milestone

text:
---

## Summary

**Milestone created:** `v28-human-run-uat` — *Human-Run UAT — manual result submission*

**Two features:**

| Feature | Scope |
|---|---|
| `human-uat-backend` | Add `UatRun.mode: Option<String>` field; `POST /api/features/{slug}/human-qa` (writes qa-results.md as Draft); `POST /api/milestone/{slug}/uat/human` (creates UatRun with mode="human") |
| `human-uat-frontend` | Shared submission modal (checklist reference → verdict selector → notes textarea); secondary "Submit manually" button in `MilestonePreparePanel.tsx` and `FeatureDetail.tsx` |

**Design decisions locked in:**
- Human path is additive — AI "Run UAT" stays primary, human link is visually subordinate
- Approval flow unchanged — agent still reviews Draft after human submits
- Feature QA output format: `## Verdict`, `## Notes`, `Runner: human (manual)`, timestamp
- Milestone mode field: `"agent"` | `"human"` string on `UatRun`

**Next:** `/sdlc-run human-uat-backend` (then `human-uat-frontend`)
